rationale: Both notes indicate strong topical overlap in governance/control mechanisms and connected-relationship contingencies: the citing paper studies buying-group monitoring/community-building with cross-level group–dyad interactions, while the cited paper studies output/process control with moderating effects from HQ–subsidiary ties. The citing paper’s stated contribution of extending interfirm governance beyond atomistic dyads makes this 2013 control/governance study a plausible substantive theory/mechanism source rather than a generic background mention, so it should be reviewed in full.
